what: `.claude/agents/research.md` is the Claude Code subagent definition for the "Research_Sub_Agent" / "Domain-Baseline Checklist Sourcer." When a new product class is encountered for the first time, it researches competitive analysis, industry standards, and open-source reference implementations (querying web sources) to DRAFT a Domain_Baseline_Checklist for that product class. It produces a draft checklist file named by product class under `baselines/` (e.g. `baselines/saas-auth.md`), presents the draft for human review, and persists the approved checklist as a version-controlled artifact linked to `feature_list.json`. Per the epistemic-integrity additions (REQ-SPEC-017 / Property 29 / Req 24.…

open_gaps.gap: The component file does not exist on disk. `.claude/agents/` contains only a 0-byte `.gitkeep`; research.md (and initializer/implementer/verifier) are unwritten. Task 10.4 is still `[ ]`. The entire component is spec-only.
open_gaps.fix: This is expected pre-implementation state (Phase 0 not started). Flag explicitly that NO agent definition file has been authored yet; the spec's claim of 'four subagent definitions with permission scopes' (PRD:17) is a spec promise, not a built artifact. Implement task 10.4 (tasks.md:218).

open_gaps.gap: REQ-SPEC-016/017 enforcement is split into a Phase-3 task (50) while the agent it governs is a Phase-0 component (10.4). The agent is BUILT (Phase 0) ~3 phases before its epistemic-integrity gate and its checklist-approval guard are wired (Phase 3 task 50). For the entire Phase 0-2 window, research.md can emit unlabeled, unfact-checked DRAFT claims and there is no guard preventing a DRAFT checklist from being used for discovery.
open_gaps.fix: Either pull the checklist-approval PreToolUse guard and the authority-tier/fact-check enforcement forward into Phase 0 (add subtasks under task 6 and 10.4), or document that REQ-SPEC-016/017 are intentionally deferred and that Phase 0-2 runs must not invoke research on an un-gated product class.

open_gaps.gap: The PreToolUse checklist-approval guard (REQ-SPEC-016 / Property 27 / design.md:213) has NO implementation subtask. Task 6.1 implements exactly four checks — check_plan_approval, check_scope_sequencing, check_artifact_guard, check_status_transition (tasks.md:132-136) — and does NOT include a check_checklist_approval. By contrast the parallel resumed-state integrity guard DID get an explicit wiring subtask (49.1). Task 50 only references the property and CHECK-12, with no subtask that adds the guard code to pre_tool_use_hook.py.
open_gaps.fix: Add a subtask under task 50 (mirroring task 49.1): 'Add check_checklist_approval(event) to pre_tool_use_hook.py — block any Initializer/discovery Write that references a checklist whose approved_at is NULL in domain_baseline_checklists; chain it with the other PreToolUse checks.' Update task 6.1's title from 'four checks' accordingly.

open_gaps.gap: Property 29's 'independent fact-check pass' and 'authority-tier labeling' have NO concrete executable component. No fact_check.py, authority_labeler, or research-claim validator appears in any component table (design.md:140-160) or task. design.md:1120 itself admits 'no Z3 check — test/runtime verified', but no runtime tool is named — only a property test (task 39.19).
open_gaps.fix: Name a concrete owner: add a tool (e.g. tools/research_claim_validator.py) or define it as a capability inside research.md's own loop with a SubagentStop/PreToolUse rejection mechanism, then cite it from Property 29 and task 50. Without it, REQ-SPEC-017 has no enforcement mechanism, only a test.

open_gaps.gap: research.md's own behavior list (design.md:273-278) and task 10.4 (tasks.md:218-221, _Requirements: 3.1, 3.2, 3.3_) omit REQ-SPEC-016, REQ-SPEC-017, AND REQ-SPEC-018 entirely. The agent that is the named subject of all three requirements does not, in its own definition, carry: (a) the do-not-emit-unlabeled-claims obligation, (b) the source-URL/authority-tier obligation, or (c) the omission_declaration field.
open_gaps.fix: Extend task 10.4's _Requirements_ line to include 3 plus REQ-SPEC-016/017/018, and add the source-URL/authority-tier/fact-check + omission_declaration behaviors to the research.md key-behaviors block in design.md:273-278.

open_gaps.gap: Property 27 mis-cites its requirement: 'Validates: Requirements 25 (REQ-SPEC-016)' (design.md:1100), but REQ-SPEC-016 is Requirement 24.1 (requirements.md:441-442). Requirement 25 is Performance/Accessibility (requirements.md:445). Dangling/incorrect cross-citation.
open_gaps.fix: Correct design.md:1100 to 'Validates: Requirement 24 (REQ-SPEC-016)'. The requirements.md cross-ref table at line 509 already correctly maps Property 27 -> Requirement 24.1.

open_gaps.gap: Migration-number for the checklist version table is asserted only in tasks.md (006_domain_baseline_checklists.sql, task 28.6) and not stated in design.md's CREATE TABLE block (design.md:498). design.md:152-153 names 007/008 as the 7th/8th tables, implying 001-006 are the original six, but the canonical migration index for domain_baseline_checklists lives only in tasks.md.
open_gaps.fix: Add the migration filename (006_domain_baseline_checklists.sql) to the design.md CREATE TABLE block at design.md:498 so the migration index is canonical in design, not only in tasks.

open_gaps.gap: No actor is specified for INSERTing the domain_baseline_checklists row (the persisted record of research.md's output). research.md has read/write to baselines/ only — 'No access to ... CI' and implicitly no DB write is granted (design.md:280). Yet a row with sha/version/approved_at must be created. Who writes it (research agent? a hook? the human approval action? the Initializer at link-time?) is unspecified.
open_gaps.fix: Specify the DB-row producer for domain_baseline_checklists — likely the human-approval action or a SessionStart/PostToolUse hook — and reconcile it against research.md's baselines/-only permission scope (research.md cannot write Postgres under its current scope).

open_gaps.gap: design.md / tasks.md coverage of research.md is THIN relative to the other three subagents and to its own three governing requirements. design.md gives it 12 lines (269-280); tasks.md gives it 4 lines (218-221). There is NO output JSON schema artifact for research (unlike feature_list.schema.json), despite REQ-SPEC-018.3 requiring 'The JSON schema for each subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration' (requirements.md:478). No checklist file-format schema is defined anywhere (what fields a baselines/*.md must contain, how claims/sources/authority-tiers are structured).
open_gaps.fix: Add a checklist artifact schema (fields per item, per-claim source/authority/fact-check structure) and a research-output JSON schema carrying omission_declaration; cite both from task 10.4 and 54. Without a defined output format, REQ-SPEC-014/017/018.3 cannot be machine-validated.

open_gaps.gap: No model / tool binding for the subagent. Unlike a runnable Claude Code agent (which needs frontmatter: name, description, tools, model), no spec file specifies which tools research.md may call (it must query web sources per design.md:275, implying WebSearch/firecrawl, but no tool grant is given) or which model runs it. grep for model:/tools:/sonnet/haiku/opus returns nothing for this agent.
open_gaps.fix: Specify research.md frontmatter in task 10.4: allowed tools (a web-search/fetch tool to satisfy 'query competitive analysis + standards + OSS refs') and a model. Note that 'read/write to baselines/ only; no CI' (design.md:280) currently contradicts the need for a web/network tool — reconcile the permission scope with the web-query behavior.

open_gaps.gap: REQ-SPEC-013..015 are cited as 'PRD references' for Requirement 3 (requirements.md:87) but are NEVER defined as individual EARS statements anywhere — the PRD requirements list (PRD:54-90) defines REQ-LOOP-005, REQ-COV-007, REQ-STATE-005, REQ-SPEC-016/017 etc. but has no REQ-SPEC-010..015 entries. They exist only as label ranges. Same for REQ-SPEC-010..012 (Requirement 2).
open_gaps.fix: Either define REQ-SPEC-013/014/015 as explicit EARS lines, or document that these IDs are a shorthand alias for Requirement 3's three acceptance criteria. Currently the ID-to-criterion mapping is implicit and unverifiable.

open_gaps.gap: Lifecycle vs requirement tension on detection of a 'new' product class. design.md:613 says research runs 'only once per product class... Re-research is triggered only by an explicit operator request', and Req 3.1 says 'WHEN a new product class is encountered for the first time' — but neither spec defines HOW 'new product class' / 'first encounter' is DETECTED (what data structure is consulted to decide a class is new). No detector component is named.
open_gaps.fix: Name the mechanism that decides a product class is 'new' (e.g. a lookup against domain_baseline_checklists by product_class) and which component performs it (the Initializer). Cite it from design.md:274 and the lifecycle.

critic_findings.detail: Property 18 (Checklist Version Link Fidelity) is entirely absent from the draft's ears_stories and properties_and_checks, yet it directly governs the linkage of research.md's primary output artifact. design.md:1018-1022: 'For any use of a domain-baseline checklist during proactive discovery, the resulting feature_list.json shall record the exact checklist_ref.path, .version, and .sha ... The recorded SHA must match the actual git blob SHA of the checklist file at the time of use.' This is the machine-checkable pin behind REQ-SPEC-015 / Requirement 3.3 — the very criterion the draft's own ears_story marks 'addressed' for 3.3. It has a dedicated property test (task 39.9, tasks.md:626-630, tag …

Requirement 28 / REQ-ORCH-004 establishes 'research' as one of the FOUR fixed baseline subagent roles (planner/implementer/verifier/research) against which optional roles are defined: requirements.md:466 'WHERE a project requires roles beyond planner/implementer/verifier/research, THE System SHALL support additional bounded subagent roles ... none SHALL have verifier write access' (PRD:64 identical). This is a requirement-level obligation that NAMES research as a permanent role with an explicit-permission-scope, never-verifier-write-access constraint — directly constraining research.md's permission model (the 'baselines/ only, no verifier write' scope the draft discusses under config_setting…

The draft does not flag that task 50's _Requirements:_ line (tasks.md:965 '_Requirements: 3.1, 3.3._') is INCOMPLETE/mis-scoped for the obligation it implements. Task 50 implements REQ-SPEC-016 AND REQ-SPEC-017 (the checklist-approval gate plus the source-URL/authority-tier/fact-check epistemic gate), but its _Requirements:_ traceability line cites only '3.1, 3.3' — which are Requirement 3's sourcing criteria, NOT Requirement 24.1 (REQ-SPEC-016) or Requirement 24.2 (REQ-SPEC-017) which task 50 actually closes. Notably, REQ-SPEC-017's source-URL/authority-tier obligation maps to Requirement 3.1 (drafting) but NOT to 3.3 (version-recording) — and the fact-check/authority gate has no acceptance…

The draft's ears_story for Requirement 24.2 / REQ-SPEC-017 asserts the fact-check enforcement 'lives only in a Phase-3 task with a property test (39.19), no implementation subtask wiring it into the agent or a hook' and elsewhere correctly notes no concrete owner exists — but it then lists 'Submit to independent fact-check (obligation)' in behaviors citing design.md:1118 as if Property 29 supplies a mechanism. More significantly, the draft's properties_and_checks claims 'Property 29 ... pins its source-URL/authority-tier/fact-check obligation' and the open_gaps suggested_fix says REQ-SPEC-017 'has no enforcement mechanism, only a test.' That is correct, but the draft OVERSTATES the test's re…

The draft marks the Z3 ears_stories for CHECK-12a/12b, CHECK-13a/13b, and CHECK-9a as status 'addressed' while only parenthetically noting they are abstract Boolean models. This understates a coverage gap the draft elsewhere argues forcefully: NONE of these Z3 checks reference research.md, the checklist artifact, claims, authority tiers, or omission_declaration concretely. Verified in harness: CHECK-12a/12b operate on bare Booleans checklistIsDraft/checklistUsedForDisc (formal_verification_merged.py:130-131,463-479); CHECK-13a/13b on omissionDeclNull/subagentAccepted13 (lines 481-500); CHECK-9a on unmappedExists/advancementAllowed annotated '[REQ-SPEC-011]' (lines 382-390) — i.e. CHECK-9a is…

The draft does not flag the migration-numbering CONTRADICTION between tasks.md and design.md for the domain_baseline_checklists table (the persistence store of research.md's output). The draft's open_gaps notes only that the migration filename '006_domain_baseline_checklists.sql' is asserted in tasks.md but absent from design.md's CREATE TABLE block — framing it as merely 'underspecified.' It is stronger than that: it is an actual numeric COLLISION. tasks.md:445 assigns domain_baseline_checklists to migration 006, but tasks.md:962 assigns requirement_versions to migration 007 and tasks.md:967 assigns gate_audit_log to 008 — while design.md:140-142 states migrations 001-006 are 'the original …

The draft's dependencies list and by_whom cite the tasks.md:779 dependency-group ('tasks: 9.2, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 10.4, 11.1') but do not surface the consequence: research.md (task 10.4) is scheduled in the SAME parallel dependency group as the verifier (10.3) and implementer (10.2), yet research.md's epistemic-integrity gate (task 50) and its checklist-approval consumer guard are Phase-3. The draft notes the Phase-0/Phase-3 split as a gap, but misses that the dependency-group at line 779 makes research.md BUILDABLE-AND-MERGEABLE in the very first parallel wave with no dependency edge to task 50, task 39.17, or task 54 — there is no ordering constraint forcing the gates to exist before resear…

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a new product class is encountered for the first time, THE System SHALL run a Research_Sub_Agent that queries competitive analysis, industry standards, and open-source reference implementations to draft a Domain_Baseline_Checklist for that product class, and SHALL present the draft for human re…
ears_needing_work.how: Core charter of research.md; encoded in agent definition (design.md:269-280), lifecycle (design.md:599-603), and task 10.4 (tasks.md:220). Behaviors: query web sources, produce draft named by product class, present for review.

ears_needing_work.criterion: IF a Domain_Baseline_Checklist is in DRAFT (not human-approved), THEN it SHALL NOT be used for discovery. (Z3 CHECK-12a/12b.)
ears_needing_work.how: Enforced NOT in research.md itself but by a PreToolUse checklist-approval guard that confirms non-null approved_at before discovery Write (design.md:213, Property 27 design.md:1094-1100). research.md is the producer of the DRAFT; the gate is the consumer-side enforcement. Z3 CHECK-12a/12b model an a…

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN the Research_Sub_Agent drafts a checklist or any sourced claim, THEN every external claim SHALL carry a source URL, each source SHALL be labeled by authority tier (primary/standard/peer-reviewed vs blog/vendor/social), and an independent fact-check pass SHALL flag unverifiable or low-authority-…
ears_needing_work.how: Directly names the Research_Sub_Agent. Mechanism described as Property 29 (design.md:1114-1120) and task 50 (tasks.md:965). BUT: research.md's own behavior list (design.md:273-278) and task 10.4 (tasks.md:218-221) do NOT mention source-URL/authority-tier/fact-check at all; the gate lives only in a P…

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a subagent (initializer, research, verifier) completes a discovery/research/verification pass, its output SHALL include a non-null omission_declaration field listing by EARS scenario category every scenario class not covered, using [Gap] markers.
ears_needing_work.how: Names 'research' as one of the three subagents whose output must carry omission_declaration. Added to schema + guard by task 54 (tasks.md:974). research.md's own definition (design.md:269-280) does NOT mention omission_declaration — only the consolidated task 54 retrofits it.

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE JSON schema for each subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration as a required, non-nullable field.
ears_needing_work.how: Task 54 adds omission_declaration to 'each subagent output schema (initializer, research, verifier)'. NOTE: no per-subagent output JSON schema file is named anywhere for research — there is a feature_list schema but no research-output schema artifact in any component table.

ears_needing_work.criterion: For any proactive-discovery pass that references a domain-baseline checklist, the checklist shall have non-null approved_at; the PreToolUse checklist-approval guard shall block discovery using an unapproved checklist. Validates Req 25 (REQ-SPEC-016), CHECK-12.
ears_needing_work.how: Guards research.md's DRAFT output from premature use. Property/test exist (task 39.17), but no implementation subtask adds the guard to pre_tool_use_hook.py. NOTE the citation says 'Requirements 25' but the requirement is Req 24.1 — internal mis-citation.

ears_needing_work.criterion: For any claim emitted by the Research subagent into a draft checklist, the claim shall carry an authority-tier label and pass the fact-check pass before human review; unlabeled/unverified claims rejected before approval. Validates Req 24 (REQ-SPEC-017). No Z3 check — test/runtime verified.
ears_needing_work.how: Property test task 39.19 (tasks.md:684-687) + task 50. No runtime mechanism component is named (no fact_check.py / authority_labeler tool in any component table); 'independent fact-check pass' has no concrete executable owner.
